summary: Buffer-library clone with import-time loader for unrelated transitive dependency
details: The package republishes the source of Feross Aboukhadijeh's `buffer` library verbatim (preserving the original MIT header) under an unrelated scope and author, with a single added top-level statement at index.js:11: `var ins = import('@sqlite-node/createsql');`. The variable is never used; the line exists solely to force-load the unpinned (`^1.1.3`) transitive dependency `@sqlite-node/createsql` whenever a consumer calls `require('@sql-access/nodesql')`. The README is malformed and refers to an unrelated 'bare-stream' project, and neither the package name nor the dependency name corresponds to any legitimate published library. This is a namespace-abuse loader: a plausible-looking buffer/SQL helper wrapper whose only on-load effect is to pull in and execute an unrelated, unpinned dependency's entry module on the installer's machine.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
